vehicles.PICKUP_FS: a generic early-2000s American full-size pickup truck (F-150 class): single cab, long bed, big chrome front bumper, simple rectangular headlights, tall boxy hood
vehicles.PICKUP_HD: a generic early-2000s heavy-duty American pickup truck (Silverado class): extended cab, short bed, wide chrome grille bar, fog lights in the bumper
vehicles.PICKUP_BIG: a generic early-2000s American pickup truck with a huge rounded crosshair-style grille (Ram class): sloped hood, flared fenders, single cab
vehicles.SEDAN_MID: a generic early-2000s Japanese mid-size family sedan (Camry class): soft rounded body, plain trapezoid grille, wraparound headlights, four doors
vehicles.SEDAN_SPORT: a generic early-2000s Japanese sporty sedan (Accord class): slightly lower stance, thin angular headlights, subtle trunk lip spoiler, four doors
vehicles.SEDAN_FULL: a generic early-2000s American full-size sedan (Taurus class): very rounded jellybean body, oval rear window, wide oval grille, four doors
vehicles.COMPACT: a generic early-2000s Japanese compact sedan (Corolla class): short hood, upright cabin, simple slim grille, small wheels, four doors
vehicles.HATCH: a generic early-2000s Japanese compact coupe (Civic class): two doors, low nose, thin headlights, small factory alloy wheels
vehicles.SUV_MID: a generic early-2000s American mid-size SUV (Explorer class): boxy two-box shape, roof rails, gray plastic lower cladding, five doors
vehicles.MINIVAN: a generic early-2000s American minivan (Caravan class): one-box egg shape, sliding rear door seam, low floor, small wheels
